title: Workshop Schema
description: Schema for workshop.json files that define container image build requirements.  Supports three document formats: single-userenv (one userenv + requirements), multi-userenv (multiple userenvs + requirements), and config-only (container configuration without requirements).

oneOf.description: Single-userenv format: defines one userenv and its build requirements.  Used for userenv definition files.
Multi-userenv format: defines requirements that target one or more named userenvs.  Used by subproject workshop.json files to specify what software to install in engine or controller images.
Config-only format: defines container configuration (entrypoint, environment, ports, labels) without build requirements.  Used for container metadata files.

workshop.description: Metadata block containing the schema version for this workshop.json file format.

version.description: The schema version this file conforms to.

userenv.description: A user environment definition that specifies the base container image and its properties.  Each userenv provides a foundation for building engine or controller images.

name.description: The userenv name, used to identify this user environment throughout crucible (e.g., 'rhubi9', 'fedora-latest', 'alma8').

label.description: A human-readable label for this userenv, used in image tags and display.

origin.description: Specifies the source container image that serves as the base for this userenv.

requires-pull-token.description: Whether pulling this base image requires authentication.

build-policy.description: Controls when the base image is pulled.  'missing' only pulls if the image is not already present locally.  'ifnewer' pulls if a newer version is available.

update-policy.description: Controls whether the base image is updated.  'always' checks for updates on every build.  'never' uses the cached version.

properties.description: Properties describing this userenv's platform support and package management.

platform.description: An array of supported CPU architectures for this userenv.

packages.description: Package management information for this userenv's base distribution.

userenvs.description: An array of userenv targets for multi-userenv workshop.json files.  Each entry maps one or more userenv names to a set of requirement indices.

items.description: A userenv target mapping, specifying which userenvs should receive which requirements.

name.description: The userenv name(s) this entry applies to.  Can be a single name or an array of names.

oneOf.description: An array of userenv names that all receive the same requirements.

requirements.description: An array of requirement name references from the top-level requirements array.  These requirements will be installed in the specified userenv(s).

items.description: A requirement name that matches a 'name' field in the requirements array.

requirements.description: An array of software installation requirements.  Each requirement specifies a named set of software to install using one of several installation methods (distro packages, pip, npm, CPAN, source builds, manual commands, or file copies).

oneOf.description: A distro-manual requirement installs distribution packages using explicit package manager commands rather than the standard install workflow.

name.description: A unique name identifying this requirement.

distro-manual_info.description: Package installation details for the distro-manual method.

packages.description: An array of package names to install using the distribution's package manager.

oneOf.description: A distro requirement installs or removes distribution packages using the standard package manager workflow.

name.description: A unique name identifying this requirement.

distro_info.description: Package installation details for the distro method.

operation.description: Whether to install or remove the specified packages.  Defaults to 'install'.

packages.description: An array of package names to install or remove.

groups.description: An array of package group names to install or remove.

environment.description: Environment variables to set during the package installation process.

oneOf.description: A manual requirement executes arbitrary shell commands during the image build.

name.description: A unique name identifying this requirement.

manual_info.description: Command execution details for the manual method.

commands.description: An array of shell commands to execute sequentially during the image build.

oneOf.description: A python3 requirement installs Python packages using pip.

name.description: A unique name identifying this requirement.

python3_info.description: Package installation details for the python3/pip method.

packages.description: An array of Python package names to install via pip.

oneOf.description: A node requirement installs Node.js packages using npm.

name.description: A unique name identifying this requirement.

node_info.description: Package installation details for the node/npm method.

packages.description: An array of Node.js package names to install via npm.

oneOf.description: A cpan requirement installs Perl modules from CPAN.

name.description: A unique name identifying this requirement.

cpan_info.description: Package installation details for the CPAN method.

packages.description: An array of Perl module names to install from CPAN.

oneOf.description: A source requirement downloads, unpacks, and builds software from source.

name.description: A unique name identifying this requirement.

source_info.description: Source build details including download URL, archive handling, and build commands.

url.description: The URL to download the source archive from.

filename.description: The local filename to save the downloaded archive as.

commands.description: Commands for unpacking, locating, and building the source.

unpack.description: The command to unpack the downloaded archive.

get_dir.description: A command that outputs the directory name created by unpacking.

commands.description: An array of build commands to execute inside the unpacked source directory.

oneOf.description: A files requirement copies files into the container image during the build.

name.description: A unique name identifying this requirement.

files_info.description: File copy details for the files method.

files.description: An array of file copy operations, each specifying a source and destination path.

src.description: The source file path on the build host.

dst.description: The destination file path inside the container image.

config.description: Container configuration options applied to the final image.  Used to set the entrypoint, environment variables, exposed ports, labels, and other container metadata.

cmd.description: The default command to execute when the container starts.

entrypoint.description: The container entrypoint as an array of command and arguments.

annotations.description: OCI image annotations as key=value strings.

envs.description: Environment variables to set in the container as key=value strings.

items.description: An environment variable in key=value format.

ports.description: TCP/UDP ports to expose from the container.

labels.description: Labels to apply to the container image.
